{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27881","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27881/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27881/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27881/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27881","id":2707932285,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA3dP86hZ8R9","number":27881,"title":"BUG: Significant difference between roots() and polyroots() function","user":{"login":"GijsB","id":5688814,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU2ODg4MTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5688814?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB","html_url":"https://github.com/GijsB","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":6359214,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MzU5MjE0","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/00%20-%20Bug","name":"00 - Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-11-30T20:16:23Z","updated_at":"2024-12-01T20:10:31Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

### Description
When I define a polynomial with a single very large root (and other small ones), I get a significant difference between the outcomes of the `roots` and `polyroots` functions. Mainly the small roots seem to differ. Maybe even more importantly, the outcomes can also become complex in the `polyroots` function.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
from numpy import roots, sort, array
from numpy.polynomial.polynomial import polyroots

p = array([0.5, -0.2, -5e+15, 0.04])
print(f'roots:     {sort(roots(p[::-1]))}') # [-1.00000000e-08  9.99999998e-09  1.25000000e+17] 
print(f'polyroots: {sort(polyroots(p))}')   # [-7.50602596e-09  7.50602647e-09  1.25000000e+17]

print()

p = array([0.5, -0.2, -5e+15, 0.05])
print(f'roots:     {sort(roots(p[::-1]))}')  # [-1.00000000e-08  9.99999998e-09  1.00000000e+17]
print(f'polyroots: {sort(polyroots(p))}') # [1.68735664e-16-2.94078821e-09j 1.68735664e-16+2.94078821e-09j ...]
```


### Error message:

_No response_

### Python and NumPy Versions:

- Numpy version: 2.1.3
- Python version: 3.13.0
- Platform: MacOS 15.1.1

### Runtime Environment:

_No response_

### Context for the issue:

I'm not 100% sure this can be considered a bug, apologies if this is not the case. The reason for reporting is that as a naive user, I expect 'new' API's to behave identical (or at least similar) to the original functions. I'm using these functions to find the intersections of 2 polynomials, which represent the performance characteristics of a propeller. 

As far as I'm concerned, this issue does not need to be prioritised in any way. I just wanted to make sure that this behaviour is known if this wasn't already the case. Thanks either way for your input/help!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27881/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27881/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2510232648","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27881#issuecomment-2510232648","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27881","id":2510232648,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86VnxxI","user":{"login":"GijsB","id":5688814,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU2ODg4MTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5688814?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB","html_url":"https://github.com/GijsB","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GijsB/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-01T20:10:30Z","updated_at":"2024-12-01T20:10:30Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Hi all,
Curiosity got the better of me on a Sunday evening. I tried to measure the root finding error for a third order polynomial, two roots symmetrically around zero (at -1 an 1) and another relatively large root. `polyroots` is in blue and `roots` is in orange.
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3305f898-34f1-4bbe-a130-ec3414225847)

Interestingly, it looks like it's very dependent on the symmetry of the 2 small roots:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/38225b8d-c272-490e-86bf-bfac13917ef8)
